Event: Bran spends hours warging into Summer despite the warning
Explanation: He indulges in addictive magic that risks losing his humanity with no shown benefit beyond escapism.

Event: Jojen warns Bran about over-warging
Explanation: He tries to protect Bran from psychological harm with no downside to himself.

Event: Bran accidentally wargs Hodor in the past and lobotomizes him
Explanation: Bran’s panicked time-warg shatters Hodor’s mind, bringing Bran no clear benefit and severely harming Hodor.

Event: Bran realizes what he did to Hodor and keeps watching
Explanation: The realization torments him emotionally while offering only grim understanding to others.

Event: Catelyn notes Bran always looks down when he lies
Explanation: She identifies his tell, helping her judge his honesty and indirectly teaching him about his own behavior.

Event: Bran sends Rickon away with Osha and Shaggydog for safety
Explanation: Sending Rickon away is meant to protect him from a dangerous journey and seems prudent with information he has.

Event: Bran chooses to accept becoming the Three-Eyed Raven
Explanation: He sacrifices personal normality for vast power and knowledge aimed at defending humanity.

Event: Bran wishes he had died instead of living crippled
Explanation: The despair severely harms his own well-being without helping anyone.

Event: Tyrion designs Bran a special saddle so he can ride
Explanation: Tyrion expends effort to restore some mobility and joy to Bran with no downside to himself.

Event: Bran follows the crow into the crypt and has a vision of Ned
Explanation: He pursues prophetic dreams that give him true foresight with no shown harm.

Event: Bran fails to know Jon is legitimate until Sam tells him
Explanation: Although a “spot check” failure, he still gains and shares crucial legitimacy info that modestly helps their cause.

Event: Three-Eyed Raven uploads all his memories into Bran despite saying Bran isn’t ready
Explanation: He overloads Bran for the greater need to preserve vital knowledge against the Night King.

Event: Bran climbs castle walls and discovers Jaime and Cersei
Explanation: The climbing desire leads to discovering a secret that gets him crippled, with no clear gain to himself but major later consequences.

Event: Jaime pushes Bran out of the window
Explanation: Jaime protects his and Cersei’s secret at the catastrophic expense of Bran’s body and future.

Event: Bran surrenders Winterfell to Theon to avoid bloodshed
Explanation: His attempt at a peaceful resolution backfires, enabling Theon’s brutal occupation and deaths with little benefit.

Event: Bran journeys to become the Three-Eyed Raven despite suffering
Explanation: He endures personal loss and pain to gain powers meant to stop existential threats to mankind.

Event: Bran is responsible for killing wildlings and Locke via warging
Explanation: He preserves himself and his group by killing foes while harming those individuals directly.

Event: Three-Eyed Raven transfers his power and knowledge into Bran, changing his personality
Explanation: The transfer saves irreplaceable knowledge and creates a new protector at the cost of Bran’s old self.

Event: Bran wargs Summer to attack wildlings in “The Rains of Castamere”
Explanation: He saves his group and himself by killing/maiming attackers, harming the wildlings.

Event: Bran wargs Hodor to kill Locke
Explanation: He gains immediate life-saving protection at the cost of Locke’s life and Hodor’s autonomy.

Event: Hodor reacts in horror after realizing Bran used his body to kill
Explanation: Hodor’s distress harms his own peace of mind and strains trust with Bran without clear benefit to anyone.

Event: Bran’s combined time-warg on Hodor causes Hodor’s brain damage
Explanation: The confusion of time-warging destroys Hodor’s higher functions without giving Bran offsetting gains.

Event: Bran’s actions reveal the Raven’s location to the Night King
Explanation: His mistake directly leads the enemy to their hideout, causing multiple deaths for no benefit.

Event: Bran’s actions indirectly cause deaths of the Raven, Summer, Children of the Forest, and Hodor
Explanation: His choices trigger a chain of fatal outcomes for key allies without improving his own position.

Event: Bran orders Rickon and Osha to seek refuge with the Umbers
Explanation: The order leads to their betrayal and deaths, nullifying the protective intent with disastrous consequences.

Event: Bran feels “My God, what have I done?” about Hodor
Explanation: The guilt damages him emotionally while only adding clarity about past harm.

Event: Bran chooses to prioritize the Long Night over exposing Littlefinger immediately
Explanation: Focusing on the existential threat while his sisters handle Littlefinger balances risks and resources well.

Event: Bran reunites with Sansa and describes her wedding night “lovely” despite her trauma
Explanation: His detached remark gives him nothing and inflicts needless pain on Sansa.

Event: Bran fails to recognize Meera’s pain and sacrifice when she leaves
Explanation: His cold dismissal hurts a loyal ally without bringing him gain.

Event: Bran forms a power trio with Sansa and Arya to expose Littlefinger
Explanation: Using his knowledge with Arya and Sansa’s skills removes a dangerous manipulator and stabilizes Northern politics.

Event: Bran trolls Littlefinger with “Chaos is a ladder”
Explanation: He unnerves a dangerous schemer, signaling omniscience and hastening Littlefinger’s downfall with minimal cost.

Event: Bran no-sells Littlefinger’s manipulations
Explanation: He remains immune to flattery and deceit, preserving his own and his house’s position.

Event: Bran reveals Littlefinger’s role in starting the conflict
Explanation: His testimony enables a just execution and removes a destabilizing force.

Event: Arya executes Littlefinger after Bran’s revelations
Explanation: The act eliminates a chronic traitor and likely prevents further bloodshed at limited risk to Arya.

Event: Bran keeps Jon’s true parentage and claim secret for a time
Explanation: Withholding the explosive truth buys time and avoids premature political chaos.

Event: Bran later shares Jon’s true parentage (with Sam)
Explanation: Revealing the truth clarifies legitimacy and alliances, albeit with mixed political fallout.

Event: Bran uses his visions to confirm Sam’s discovery that Jon is legitimate
Explanation: He validates crucial legal information that strengthens Jon’s rightful claim.

Event: Bran stares down Theon calmly when Theon has taken Winterfell
Explanation: His composure unsettles Theon and asserts moral authority without material cost.

Event: Bran abdicates effective lordship of Winterfell to fulfill Three-Eyed Raven role
Explanation: He relinquishes personal power to someone more suited to rule while he focuses on a larger existential duty.

Event: Bran chooses not to use his powers to micromanage Cersei’s plans
Explanation: He avoids overreach and paradoxes, trusting allies to handle conventional politics while he addresses the mystical threat.

Event: Bran accepts being voted King Bran the Broken despite not wanting power
Explanation: He assumes a stabilizing, beneficial role for Westeros at some personal cost to his autonomy.

Event: Bran claims his responsibility as Three-Eyed Raven overrules any claim to Winterfell
Explanation: He sacrifices a lesser title to honor a greater, more impactful duty.

Event: Bran waits all day in the courtyard to be the first person Jaime sees
Explanation: He stages a psychologically powerful confrontation that reinforces accountability and likely deters further wrongdoing.

Event: Bran repeats “the things we do for love” to Jaime at his trial
Explanation: He forces Jaime to confront past sins and ensures the council has full context without derailing the alliance.

Event: Bran uses his advanced knowledge to act as “the Smart Guy” for the Starks
Explanation: His information and insight repeatedly guide better decisions for his house and allies.

Event: Bran effectively abdicates Winterfell’s rule so Sansa can govern
Explanation: He backs the more politically adept sibling, leading to more stable regional governance.

Event: Bran commands respect as a young acting Lord of Winterfell
Explanation: He governs competently despite age and disability, benefitting himself and his people.

Event: Bran accepts the title “Bran the Broken”
Explanation: Accepting a humbling but unifying title reinforces his legitimacy and thematic acceptance of his past.
